more.title: Unica Plastics Supports South African Venues With Locally Moulded Seating
more.description: With South Africa moving through the cooler stretch of 2026, a busy run of weddings, conferences, school functions and community events keeps demand strong for seating that moves easily, stores neatly and lasts. Unica Plastics, a Pretoria-based maker of injection-moulded plastic goods, is answering that demand with a locally produced range of chairs, desks and [...]

more.title: Damaged Cars Wanted Buys Non-Running Gauteng Vehicles for Cash This Winter
more.description: With Gauteng now in the coldest stretch of July, households across the province are once again eyeing the accident-damaged or non-running car that has been sitting idle in the driveway. more.title: Higher Interest Rates: A Growing Threat to South African Businesses
more.description: Interest rates play a critical role in determining the health and growth prospects of an economy. When central banks increase interest rates to combat inflation and stabilise currency markets, the unintended consequences for businesses can be significant. In South Africa, where economic growth remains subdued, unemployment persistently high and business confidence fragile, sustained higher interest [...]

more.description: As interest in new energy mobility continues to grow in South Africa, BYD Auto South Africa and Absa have elevated their collaboration through BYD Finance, a product of Absa, a strategic cooperation that builds on the relationship first established in 2025. more.title: Mandela Day ideas: 7 simple ways to give back from home in South Africa
more.description: As Mandela Day draws closer on 18 July, WWF South Africa affirms there are ways for your 67 minutes to make a meaningful difference from home, Cape {town} Etc reports. While community clean-ups, food drives and volunteering events remain an important part of the annual tradition, not everyone has the flexibility to attend organised initiatives.
